summary: PR 2718 updated the repository configure script to add explicit --bugfix and --asm build flags. --debug, --bugfix, and --asm now imply --non-matching; --bugfix appends -DBUGFIX to base C flags; and asm directory use is controlled directly by --asm rather than by the broader non-matching mode. The rest of the diff is Python formatting/trailing-comma cleanup in object lists.

key_files.role: Only changed file; adds build flags and adjusts non-matching/asm behavior plus formatting cleanup.
evidence_refs: raw_diff configure.py hunks around parser.add_argument, args.parse_args, config.asm_dir, cflags_base

what_changed.text: Added --bugfix as a store_true configure flag with help text saying it builds with bug fixes and implies --non-matching.
Added --asm as a store_true configure flag with help text saying it overrides src files with asm equivalents and implies --non-matching.
After parsing arguments, if any of args.debug, args.bugfix, or args.asm is true, args.non_matching is set true.
Changed asm directory disabling logic from checking config.non_matching to checking args.asm, so asm sources are only built when --asm is explicitly supplied.
When --bugfix is supplied, configure appends -DBUGFIX to cflags_base.
Applied formatting cleanup to several Object(...) calls and list syntax in MatchingFor sections without changing object paths or matching categories in the shown diff.

smart_moves.text: The PR separates the meaning of non-matching mode from asm override selection: debug and bugfix builds still imply non-matching, but asm replacement now requires the more specific --asm flag.
The bugfix mode is represented as a compiler define (-DBUGFIX), keeping source-level conditional bugfix code controlled from configure.py.

decomp_lessons.text: For this build system, non-matching mode is a broader compatibility/build mode that can be implied by feature flags such as --debug, --bugfix, and --asm; asm source replacement is a separate opt-in behavior controlled by --asm.

naming_conventions.text: New configure flags use lower-case argparse long options matching the generated args attribute names: --bugfix -> args.bugfix and --asm -> args.asm.
The bugfix compiler switch is named BUGFIX and is passed as -DBUGFIX.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.text: Use --asm when a build should override C source files with asm equivalents; do not rely on --non-matching alone for asm replacement after this change.
Use --bugfix to enable code guarded by BUGFIX while also entering non-matching mode.

notes: Evidence is strong for changed files and code behavior because the full diff for the only changed file is loaded.
There are no PR body details, issue comments, reviews, or review comments in the slice, so reviewer intent and validation results are unavailable.
No build, objdiff, or regression output is present; no validation result is inferred.
Formatting-only hunks are identified from the diff shape but not assigned broader intent beyond cleanup.

accepted_candidate_records.title: configure.py separates non-matching mode from asm override
accepted_candidate_records.text: After PR 2718, --debug, --bugfix, and --asm imply --non-matching, but asm source replacement is controlled specifically by --asm through args.asm/config.asm_dir behavior.

accepted_candidate_records.title: --bugfix maps to -DBUGFIX
accepted_candidate_records.text: The --bugfix configure flag appends -DBUGFIX to cflags_base and also implies --non-matching.

source_update_candidates.title: configure.py build flags added in PR 2718
source_update_candidates.text: configure.py supports --bugfix and --asm. --debug, --bugfix, and --asm imply --non-matching. --bugfix appends -DBUGFIX; --asm controls whether config.asm_dir remains enabled.
source_update_candidates.reason: Path-scoped build-tooling behavior is directly evidenced by the diff and may be useful for future configure.py or build-mode searches.

rejection_notes: Do not infer that --bugfix or --asm were tested; no CI, build log, or validation output is included.
